sections.name: 大問2
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bを読み，その文意にそって(26)から(31)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: The Hanging Gardens of Babylon
paragraphs: In the fifth century BC, lists of the world's most impressive works of art and architecture began appearing in Greek texts. The most famous such list describes seven particularly amazing sites. Only one of these "Seven Wonders of the Ancient World" — the Pyramids of Giza, in Egypt — survives today. ( 26 ), historians and archaeologists have discovered sufficient evidence to confirm that five more actually existed. The seventh, however, remains a mystery: the Hanging Gardens of Babylon.

The gardens were long thought to have been constructed by King Nebuchadnezzar II in the city of Babylon, in present-day Iraq. Written records from the time of Nebuchadnezzar's rule, however, ( 27 ). The gardens have been mentioned in various ancient texts, which describe a tall, multileveled structure lined with vegetation, but these were all produced centuries after the gardens were said to have been built. Without any firsthand records of the gardens' construction, archaeologists have been unable to locate any ruins in the area that prove they ever existed.

Research by scholar Stephanie Dalley suggests that efforts to find the gardens ( 28 ). Dalley translated texts written by a king named Sennacherib, who lived a century before Nebuchadnezzar. The texts describe impressive raised gardens at Sennacherib's palace. Sennacherib, however, ruled in the city of Nineveh, 300 miles from Babylon. Dalley notes that Sennacherib also constructed complex systems for transporting water into Nineveh, and she believes these could have been used to maintain the gardens there. If Dalley is correct, ancient descriptions of the Hanging Gardens of Babylon may actually refer to the gardens at Sennacherib's palace.
translations: 紀元前5世紀にギリシャの文献に世界で最も印象的な芸術・建築作品のリストが現れ始めた。最も有名なリストは特に素晴らしい7つの場所を記述する。「世界の七不思議」のうち現存するのはエジプトのギザのピラミッドのみだ。( 26 )、歴史家と考古学者は5つがさらに実在した十分な証拠を発見した。しかし7番目はバビロンの空中庭園で謎のままだ。
庭園は現イラクのバビロンでネブカドネザル2世により建設されたと考えられてきた。しかしネブカドネザル統治時代の文書は庭園について( 27 )。庭園は様々な古代文献で言及されているが、建設されたとされる数世紀後に作成されたものだ。一次資料がないため考古学者は存在を証明する遺跡を見つけられていない。
学者ステファニー・ダリーの研究によると庭園の探索は( 28 )可能性がある。ダリーはネブカドネザルの1世紀前に生きたセンナケリブ王の文献を翻訳した。文献にはセンナケリブの宮殿の印象的な高架庭園が記されていた。センナケリブはバビロンから300マイル離れたニネベを統治していた。

5つがさらに実在した十分な証拠を発見した
Written records make no mention of the gardens

efforts may be focusing on the wrong location

passages.label: B
passages.title: Aquaculture and Wild Fish Stocks
paragraphs: For decades, the world's wild fish populations have been declining, mainly due to overfishing. As the situation has become increasingly serious, it has often been suggested that aquaculture, also known as fish farming, should be promoted as an alternative to commercial fishing. It appears, however, that the aquaculture industry has ( 29 ). In a recent study, researchers analyzed historical data on both aquaculture and traditional fishing over a 44-year period. In eight out of nine cases, aquaculture did nothing to relieve the pressure on wild fish populations, despite having greatly increased the production of farmed fish.

The idea that a resource can be conserved by using another, more easily replaceable one seems logical. However, cases in various industries suggest that the opposite is true. For example, it was once assumed that replacing traditional energy sources with renewable ones would lower demand for fossil fuels, but the increased supply has actually led to greater overall energy consumption. ( 30 ), it is now believed that the availability of farmed fish has merely encouraged people to consume fish in greater quantities.

There is also another problem related to aquaculture. For years, many aquaculture companies have focused on farming species such as salmon and tuna, which unfortunately have to be fed smaller fish that are caught in the wild. Experts say this problem ( 31 ), however. By focusing on farming types of fish that eat algae or other common plantlike organisms, fish farms can become more ecologically friendly instead of making the situation worse.
translations: 何十年もの間世界の天然魚の個体数は主に乱獲により減少してきた。水産養殖は商業漁業の代替として推進されるべきと提案されてきた。しかし養殖業は( 29 )ようだ。研究者が44年間のデータを分析した結果、9例中8例で養殖は天然魚への圧力を緩和しなかった。
ある資源をより代替可能な別のもので保全するという発想は論理的に見える。しかし様々な産業の事例は逆が真であることを示している。例えば再生可能エネルギーで化石燃料需要が下がると想定されたが実際は全体消費が増加した。( 30 )、養殖魚の入手可能性は人々により多くの魚を消費するよう促しただけと考えられている。
養殖にはもう一つの問題がある。サーモンやマグロの養殖には天然の小さな魚を餌にする必要がある。しかし専門家はこの問題は( 31 )と述べる。藻や植物系有機体を食べる魚種を養殖すれば環境に優しくなれる。

sections.name: 大問3
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，B，Cの内容に関して，質問に対して最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: The Rebirth of the Scottish Whiskey Industry
paragraphs: During the 1980s, the Scottish whiskey industry was in a slump. Falling sales led to the closure of a number of long-established whiskey makers, and many experts believed the decline to be irreversible. At that time, rival beverages, such as vodka and rum, were aggressively marketed, so they became fashionable among younger people. Prior to the 1980s, the custom of consuming whiskey had been passed down from generation to generation. This had led the whiskey industry to assume its consumer base was guaranteed, but the younger generation became less likely to follow family traditions, and the industry paid a heavy price in the form of falling sales.

A further problem was that the Scottish whiskey industry had greatly increased production in the 1970s. This was risky because whiskey requires aging, a process that can take several decades. The aging process made adjusting production to meet demand a near impossibility. By the 1980s, the falling demand for whiskey, which was made worse by an economic downturn, had created a massive surplus. Fierce competition between whiskey manufacturers led to severe price cuts and the manufacturing of new, lower-quality products that harmed the industry's overall reputation. Even famous manufacturers with long-established reputations for excellence went out of business.

Fortunately, the situation was not permanent. Scottish whiskey producers started focusing on promoting high-quality products known as single-malt whiskeys. Also important were their efforts to inform consumers about the merits of these whiskeys, why they commanded a higher price, and how they could be paired with food or cigars. This strategy was highly successful and encouraged consumers abroad to open their wallets for premium Scottish whiskeys. This, in turn, led to whiskey makers in other nations imitating the strategy and creating their own luxury brands. Today, interest in and demand for whiskey has never been stronger.
translations: 1980年代、スコッチウイスキー産業は不況だった。売上減少で老舗メーカーが閉鎖され衰退は不可逆的と信じられた。ウォッカやラムなどのライバル飲料が積極的に宣伝され若者に流行した。1980年代以前はウイスキー消費の習慣が世代から世代へ受け継がれていたため消費者基盤が保証されていると想定していたが若い世代は家族の伝統に従わなくなった。
さらに1970年代にスコッチウイスキー産業は生産を大幅に増やした。ウイスキーは熟成に数十年かかるため生産調整がほぼ不可能だった。1980年代までに需要減少と不景気で大量の余剰が生じた。激しい競争で大幅な値下げと低品質製品が生まれ業界全体の評判を損ねた。

passages.label: B
passages.title: Richard III
paragraphs: In 2012, the body of Richard III, king of England from 1483 to 1485, was found beneath a parking lot in the English city of Leicester. Richard was the subject of one of William Shakespeare's most well-known plays and was one of England's most infamous rulers. He is commonly remembered as a physically disabled man who was desperate to become king and murdered his brother and two nephews in order to achieve his goal.

Richard's reputation does not originate in Shakespeare's play, but further back, in Thomas More's History of King Richard III. Modern-day experts view many of the details in More's book as highly questionable, since they were written in support of the family that won the throne from Richard, but the book's portrayal of Richard as evil became the basis for the negative reputation of him that remains to this day.

The Richard III Society, formed in 1924 with the aim of researching the king's life, strongly disputes the common image of Richard. In the hope of restoring his reputation, the society helped sponsor the research that led to the discovery of his body, and some of the findings of the society and other researchers have been particularly eye-opening. According to analysis of the skeleton, it turns out that Richard's reported physical disabilities were largely a myth. In fact, some of the injuries observed on the bones suggest that Richard likely fought in battle, which supports historical reports that suggest he was a skilled soldier.

Debate continues, however, about how Richard became king and the things he did during his two years in power. The Richard III Society points to his notable social and political reforms and claims that he is innocent of the murders that were the source of his unfortunate reputation. However, while acknowledging that some of Richard's policies were beneficial, many historians believe he was far from a generous or caring king and may very well have committed cruel acts. In the end, it is probably wisest to see Richard as a ruler who operated in a manner typical of the time in which he lived, and to realize that exactly how he came to power may not really matter that much.
translations: 2012年にイングランド王リチャード3世（1483-1485年在位）の遺体がレスターの駐車場の下から発見された。リチャードはシェイクスピアの最も有名な戯曲の題材であり身体障害者で権力欲が強く兄と甥2人を殺害した人物として記憶されている。
リチャードの評判はシェイクスピアの劇ではなくトマス・モアの『リチャード3世の歴史』に由来する。現代の専門家はモアの書の詳細の多くを疑わしいと見ている。リチャードから王位を奪った家族を支持するために書かれたからだ。しかしリチャードを悪として描いた内容が今日まで続く否定的評判の基礎となった。
1924年設立のリチャード3世協会はリチャードの悪いイメージに異議を唱えている。骨格の分析では報告された身体障害はほぼ神話であり骨の傷は戦闘に参加したことを示し熟練した兵士だったという歴史的報告を裏付ける。
しかしリチャードがどのように王になったかについての議論は続く。リチャード3世協会は社会的・政治的改革を指摘するが多くの歴史家は寛大でも思いやりのある王でもなく残酷な行為をした可能性があると考えている。結局、当時の典型的な方法で統治した支配者と見るのが賢明だろう。

passages.label: C
passages.title: The Temples of Jayavarman VII
paragraphs: At the height of King Jayavarman VII's rule, his Khmer empire covered most of Southeast Asia, with its center at Angkor, in present-day Cambodia. Prior to Jayavarman's rule, the political situation in the region had been unstable as a result of ongoing military struggles between shifting alliances of powerful local warlords, as well as battles between these alliances and the neighboring Cham people. After a Cham invasion defeated the Khmer empire's previous ruler, however, Jayavarman and his allies not only drove out the invaders but also managed to crush other warlords who hoped to rule the empire themselves. Jayavarman gained the throne in 1181.

Jayavarman's rule, which lasted for more than 30 years, brought peace and prosperity to the region, but he also seems to have been obsessed with constructing as many Buddhist temples as possible during his reign. Though promotion of religion had long been a fundamental part of Khmer culture, Jayavarman took it to a whole new level, building temples faster and in greater numbers than any previous king. Some researchers suggest he did this because he felt his time may be limited — he became king relatively late in life, at the age of 61, and he suffered from a long-term medical condition.

Jayavarman was a passionate follower of Buddhism, which was reflected in his concern for the well-being of his people. In addition to the many temples he built, he also built over a hundred hospitals, each employing doctors, pharmacists, and other healthcare professionals. The quality of care was advanced for the time: pulse readings were used to aid diagnoses, and butter and honey were prescribed as medicines. Supplies from the government arrived at these hospitals frequently, and it appears that any citizen in the empire, regardless of income or social standing, was eligible for treatment at no cost.

While Jayavarman's time as king is considered by many to be the Khmer empire's golden age, it may also have paved the way for the empire's downfall. According to some researchers, Jayavarman's temple construction was evidence of his policy of centralizing power. As the king himself took ownership of the temples' lands, a unified, government-controlled system emerged, depriving local landowners of power. Meanwhile, construction of the temples required that tens of thousands of people relocate to cities, which meant there were far fewer people in rural areas to farm the land and produce food for the empire. Furthermore, the building projects used up a significant amount of the empire's wealth. These factors combined became a huge problem for later Khmer kings when the empire suffered because of droughts and monsoons.
translations: ジャヤヴァルマン7世の統治の絶頂期、クメール帝国は東南アジアのほとんどを覆い、現カンボジアのアンコールを中心とした。ジャヤヴァルマン以前は地方軍閥間の軍事闘争とチャム族との戦いで不安定だった。チャム族の侵略で前王が倒された後、ジャヤヴァルマンと同盟者は侵略者を追い出し他の軍閥も制圧した。
30年以上続いた統治は平和と繁栄をもたらしたが、彼は在位中にできるだけ多くの仏教寺院を建設することに執着していたようだ。61歳で比較的遅く即位し長期の健康問題を抱えていたため時間が限られていると感じていた可能性がある。
熱心な仏教徒であり国民の福祉を気にかけた。100以上の病院を建設し脈拍測定やバター・蜂蜜の処方など当時としては進んだ医療を提供した。収入や社会的地位に関係なく全市民が無料で治療を受けられた。
しかしジャヤヴァルマンの時代はクメール帝国の衰退への道を切り開いた可能性もある。寺院建設は権力の中央集権化の証拠であり地方地主の権力を奪った。数万人が都市へ移住させられ農村の労働力が減少し帝国の富も消費された。これらの要因は後のクメール王が干ばつやモンスーンに直面した時の大問題となった。

focusPoints.title: 仮定法過去完了 could have pp + 推量 may be -ing
focusPoints.subtitle: Past Modal Perfect and Present Speculation
focusPoints.explanation: could have + pp は「〜した可能性がある/〜できたかもしれない」と過去の可能性や推量を表す。may be + -ing は「〜しているかもしれない」と現在の推量を表す。歴史的考察に頻出。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: She believes these could have been used to maintain the gardens there.

examples.en: She believes these could have been used to maintain the gardens there.
examples.ja: 彼女はこれらがそこの庭園を維持するために使われた可能性があると信じている。
examples.note: could have been used: 過去の可能性の推量。「使われたかもしれない」

examples.en: Research by scholar Stephanie Dalley suggests that efforts to find the gardens may be focusing on the wrong location.
examples.ja: 学者ステファニー・ダリーの研究は、庭園を見つける努力が間違った場所に焦点を当てているかもしれないと示唆している。
examples.note: suggests that + may be focusing: 研究に基づく推量。may be + -ing で現在進行形の推量

examples.en: The ancient builders could have used advanced techniques unknown to us today.
examples.ja: 古代の建設者たちは、今日私たちには知られていない高度な技術を使った可能性がある。
examples.note: could have used: 過去の可能性・推量の基本形

practiceQuestions.q: 'could have been used' はどのような意味ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「使われた可能性がある」。could have + pp は過去の可能性の推量。水輸送システムが庭園維持に使われた"かもしれない"。確実ではないが可能性を示す。

practiceQuestions.q: 'may be focusing on the wrong location' の時制は何ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: may be + -ing で現在進行形の推量。「（現在）間違った場所に焦点を当てているかもしれない」。研究者たちの現在の行動に対する推量。

practiceQuestions.q: 'If Dalley is correct, ancient descriptions...may actually refer to' の構文は何ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: If + 現在形(条件) + may + V(推量的帰結)。仮定法ではなく直説法。ダリーの説が正しい場合の可能性を述べている。

practiceQuestions.q: ダリーの主張の根拠を2つ答えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: ①センナケリブのテキストに見事な高架庭園の記述がある ②センナケリブがニネヴェに水輸送の複雑なシステムを建設した。庭園維持に使われた可能性がある。

highlightPatterns: may be focusing on the wrong location
could have been used to maintain the gardens

practicePassage.en:
[出典: The Hanging Gardens of Babylon]
Research by scholar Stephanie Dalley suggests that efforts to find the gardens may be focusing on the wrong location. Dalley translated texts written by a king named Sennacherib, who lived a century before Nebuchadnezzar. The texts describe impressive raised gardens at Sennacherib's palace. Sennacherib, however, ruled in the city of Nineveh, 300 miles from Babylon. Dalley notes that Sennacherib also constructed complex systems for transporting water into Nineveh, and she believes these could have been used to maintain the gardens there. If Dalley is correct, ancient descriptions of the Hanging Gardens of Babylon may actually refer to the gardens at Sennacherib's palace.
practicePassage.ja: 学者ステファニー・ダリーの研究は、庭園を見つける努力が間違った場所に焦点を当てているかもしれないと示唆している。ダリーはネブカドネザルの1世紀前に生きていたセンナケリブという王が書いたテキストを翻訳した。そのテキストにはセンナケリブの宮殿にある見事な高架庭園が記述されている。しかしセンナケリブはバビロンから300マイル離れたニネヴェの都市を統治していた。ダリーはセンナケリブがニネヴェに水を輸送する複雑なシステムも建設したことに注目し、これらがそこの庭園を維持するために使われた可能性があると信じている。ダリーが正しければ、バビロンの空中庭園に関する古代の記述は実際にはセンナケリブの宮殿の庭園を指しているかもしれない。

focusPoints.title: despite + 完了動名詞（譲歩の逆説）
focusPoints.subtitle: Despite + Perfect Gerund for Concession
focusPoints.explanation: despite + having + pp は「〜したにもかかわらず」と完了した事実に反する結果を述べる譲歩表現。期待に反する結果を強調する高度な構文。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Aquaculture did nothing to relieve the pressure on wild fish populations, despite having greatly increased the production of farmed fish.

examples.en: In eight out of nine cases, aquaculture did nothing to relieve the pressure on wild fish populations, despite having greatly increased the production of farmed fish.
examples.ja: 9件中8件で、養殖魚の生産を大幅に増加させたにもかかわらず、養殖は天然魚の資源への圧力を軽減するのに何の役にも立たなかった。
examples.note: despite having greatly increased: 完了動名詞の譲歩。「大幅に増加させたにもかかわらず」

examples.en: It has often been suggested that aquaculture should be promoted as an alternative to commercial fishing.
examples.ja: 養殖は商業漁業の代替として推進されるべきだとしばしば提案されてきた。
examples.note: It has been suggested that + should: 非人称受動態+提案。have been + pp で経験・継続。

examples.en: Despite having studied all night, she failed the exam.
examples.ja: 一晩中勉強したにもかかわらず、試験に落ちた。
examples.note: despite + having pp: 完了動名詞の譲歩の基本形

practiceQuestions.q: 'despite having greatly increased' の 'having increased' はなぜ完了動名詞ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 主文の動詞(did nothing)より前に完了した行為を示す。「(すでに)大幅に増加させた」という完了した事実にもかかわらず。despite + -ing ではただの同時性になる。

practiceQuestions.q: 'it has often been suggested that aquaculture should be promoted' の構文を分析してください。
practiceQuestions.a: It(形式主語) + has been suggested(現在完了受動態) + that + should be promoted(提案のshould)。「〜すべきとしばしば提案されてきた」。

practiceQuestions.q: 養殖が天然魚保護に役立たなかった理由を研究結果から説明してください。
practiceQuestions.a: 9件中8件で養殖魚の生産増加が天然魚への圧力を軽減しなかった。養殖は代替ではなく、総消費量の増加をもたらした（次段落で詳述）。

practiceQuestions.q: 'the aquaculture industry has not had the effect that was intended' の 'that was intended' は何を修飾していますか？
practiceQuestions.a: the effect(効果)を修飾する関係代名詞。「意図された効果」=天然魚保護。実際には期待通りの効果がなかった。

highlightPatterns: despite having greatly increased the production of farmed fish

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Aquaculture and Wild Fish Stocks]
For decades, the world's wild fish populations have been declining, mainly due to overfishing. As the situation has become increasingly serious, it has often been suggested that aquaculture, also known as fish farming, should be promoted as an alternative to commercial fishing. It appears, however, that the aquaculture industry has not had the effect that was intended. In a recent study, researchers analyzed historical data on both aquaculture and traditional fishing over a 44-year period. In eight out of nine cases, aquaculture did nothing to relieve the pressure on wild fish populations, despite having greatly increased the production of farmed fish.
practicePassage.ja: 数十年にわたり、世界の天然魚の個体数は主に乱獲のために減少してきた。状況がますます深刻になるにつれ、養殖（魚の養殖）を商業漁業の代替として推進すべきだとしばしば提案されてきた。しかし、養殖産業は意図された効果をもたらしていないようだ。最近の研究で、研究者たちは44年間にわたる養殖と伝統的な漁業の両方の歴史的データを分析した。9件中8件で、養殖魚の生産を大幅に増加させたにもかかわらず、養殖は天然魚の資源への圧力を軽減するのに何の役にも立たなかった。

focusPoints.title: 譲歩の while + -ing + 主張の believe/may
focusPoints.subtitle: Concessive While-Clause with Hedged Claims
focusPoints.explanation: while + -ing は「〜を認めつつも」と部分的に認めた上で反論する譲歩表現。believe + may は「〜かもしれない」と慎重な主張を示す。議論文のバランスを取る高度な構文。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: While acknowledging that some of Richard's policies were beneficial, many historians believe he was far from a generous or caring king.

examples.en: While acknowledging that some of Richard's policies were beneficial, many historians believe he was far from a generous or caring king and may very well have committed cruel acts.
examples.ja: リチャードの政策のいくつかが有益であったことを認めつつも、多くの歴史家は彼が寛大で思いやりのある王からは程遠く、残酷な行為を犯した可能性が十分にあると信じている。
examples.note: while acknowledging(譲歩) + believe(主張) + may have committed(推量): 3段階の議論構造

examples.en: In the end, it is probably wisest to see Richard as a ruler who operated in a manner typical of the time in which he lived.
examples.ja: 結局、リチャードを彼が生きた時代に典型的な方法で統治した支配者と見なすのがおそらく最も賢明である。
examples.note: it is wisest to V: 形式主語+最上級。in which he lived: 関係代名詞+前置詞

examples.en: While admitting the plan has merits, the committee decided against it.
examples.ja: その計画に利点があることを認めつつも、委員会はそれに反対する決定をした。
examples.note: While admitting: 譲歩+分詞。「認めつつも」

practiceQuestions.q: 'While acknowledging that...many historians believe' の論理構造を説明してください。
practiceQuestions.a: while(譲歩) + acknowledging(部分的承認) + believe(主張)。「有益な政策もあったことは認めるが、それでも寛大な王ではなかった」。部分承認→反論のパターン。

practiceQuestions.q: 'may very well have committed cruel acts' の 'very well' はどのような役割ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: mayの可能性を強調する副詞句。「十分に〜した可能性がある」。単なるmay(かもしれない)より可能性が高いことを示す。

practiceQuestions.q: 'far from a generous or caring king' の 'far from' はどのような意味ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「〜からは程遠い」。寛大で思いやりのある王とは全く違ったという強い否定。far from + 形容詞/名詞: 強い否定表現。

practiceQuestions.q: 筆者の最終的な結論は何ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: リチャードを時代に典型的な統治者として見るのが最も賢明(probably wisest)。善悪の極端な評価ではなく、時代的文脈で理解すべき。

highlightPatterns: while acknowledging that some of Richard's policies were beneficial, many historians believe he was far from

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Richard III]
Debate continues, however, about how Richard became king and the things he did during his two years in power. The Richard III Society points to his notable social and political reforms and claims that he is innocent of the murders that were the source of his unfortunate reputation. However, while acknowledging that some of Richard's policies were beneficial, many historians believe he was far from a generous or caring king and may very well have committed cruel acts. In the end, it is probably wisest to see Richard as a ruler who operated in a manner typical of the time in which he lived, and to realize that exactly how he came to power may not really matter that much.
practicePassage.ja: しかし、リチャードがどのようにして王になったか、そして在位2年間に何をしたかについての議論は続いている。リチャード3世協会は彼の注目すべき社会的・政治的改革を指摘し、不幸な評判の原因となった殺人について彼は無実だと主張する。しかし、リチャードの政策のいくつかが有益であったことを認めつつも、多くの歴史家は彼が寛大で思いやりのある王からは程遠く、残酷な行為を犯した可能性が十分にあると信じている。結局、リチャードを彼が生きた時代に典型的な方法で統治した支配者と見なすのがおそらく最も賢明であり、彼がどのようにして権力を握ったかは実際にはそれほど重要ではないかもしれないと認識すべきだ。

focusPoints.title: believe + O + to be C（SVOC推量構文）
focusPoints.subtitle: SVOC Pattern with Infinitive
focusPoints.explanation: believe/consider/assume + O + to be + C は「OがCであると信じる/考える」。S + believed + O + to be + C の受動態は O + was believed + to be + C。推量や判断を述べる高度な構文。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Many experts believed the decline to be irreversible.

examples.en: Many experts believed the decline to be irreversible.
examples.ja: 多くの専門家はその衰退が不可逆的であると信じていた。
examples.note: believed(V) + the decline(O) + to be irreversible(C): SVOC構文

examples.en: This had led the whiskey industry to assume its consumer base was guaranteed.
examples.ja: これにより、ウイスキー業界は消費者基盤が保証されていると思い込んでいた。
examples.note: had led(V) + the industry(O) + to assume(C): lead+O+to V使役。assume以下も入れ子のSVOC。

examples.en: Scientists consider the evidence to be inconclusive.
examples.ja: 科学者たちはその証拠は決定的でないと考えている。
examples.note: consider + O + to be + C: SVOC推量の基本形

practiceQuestions.q: 'believed the decline to be irreversible' の構文を分析してください。
practiceQuestions.a: believed(V) + the decline(O) + to be irreversible(C)。SVOC構文。「衰退が不可逆的であると信じた」。= believed that the decline was irreversible のthat節を圧縮した形。

practiceQuestions.q: 'This had led the whiskey industry to assume' の 'had led' はなぜ過去完了ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 1980年代以前(より前の時点)から業界が思い込んでいた→1980年代の衰退という時系列。過去のある時点より前の継続的影響を示す。

practiceQuestions.q: ウイスキー産業が衰退した主な原因を2つ答えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: ①ライバル飲料(ウォッカ、ラム)の積極的マーケティングにより若者の嗜好が変化 ②若い世代が家族の伝統(ウイスキーを飲む習慣)に従わなくなった。

practiceQuestions.q: 'paid a heavy price in the form of falling sales' はどのような比喩表現ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「大きな代償を払った」。pay a heavy price: 重大な結果を被る。in the form of: 「〜という形で」代償の具体的内容(売上減少)を示す。

highlightPatterns: believed the decline to be irreversible

practicePassage.en:
[出典: The Rebirth of the Scottish Whiskey Industry]
During the 1980s, the Scottish whiskey industry was in a slump. Falling sales led to the closure of a number of long-established whiskey makers, and many experts believed the decline to be irreversible. At that time, rival beverages, such as vodka and rum, were aggressively marketed, so they became fashionable among younger people. Prior to the 1980s, the custom of consuming whiskey had been passed down from generation to generation. This had led the whiskey industry to assume its consumer base was guaranteed, but the younger generation became less likely to follow family traditions, and the industry paid a heavy price in the form of falling sales.
practicePassage.ja: 1980年代、スコットランドのウイスキー産業は不振だった。売上減少により老舗ウイスキーメーカーが多数閉鎖され、多くの専門家はその衰退が不可逆的であると信じていた。当時、ウォッカやラムなどのライバル飲料が積極的に宣伝され、若者の間で流行した。1980年代以前は、ウイスキーを飲む習慣は世代から世代へ受け継がれていた。これにより、ウイスキー業界は消費者基盤が保証されていると思い込んでいたが、若い世代は家族の伝統に従う可能性が低くなり、業界は売上減少という形で大きな代償を払った。

focusPoints.title: 今回の重要なパラフレーズ
focusPoints.subtitle: Key Paraphrases in This Exam
focusPoints.explanation: 選択肢の正答は本文表現の言い換えです。同じ意味を異なる言葉で表現する力が合否を分けます。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
①outweigh = exceed in importance
②spacious = roomy, large
③nurture = cultivate, foster
④weary = tired, exhausted
⑤sustain = maintain, keep up

examples.en: assumed its consumer base was guaranteed → believed younger people would always consume whiskey
examples.ja: 消費者基盤が保証されていると想定→若者が常にウイスキーを消費すると信じた

examples.en: adjusting production was a near impossibility → difficulties in predicting what quantity could be sold
examples.ja: 生産調整がほぼ不可能→販売量予測の困難
examples.note: 抽象化→具体化のパラフレーズ。

examples.en: operated in a manner typical of the time → no better or worse than other kings
examples.ja: 当時の典型的な方法で統治→他の王と比べて良くも悪くもない

practiceQuestions.q: 'considered by many to be the Khmer empire's golden age' を言い換えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: 'widely regarded as the peak period of the Khmer civilization'。golden age = peak period / zenith。

practiceQuestions.q: 'may also have contributed to its decline' を言い換えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: 'might have played a role in the empire's fall'。contributed to = played a role in。decline = fall / collapse。

practiceQuestions.q: 'economic burden' を別の表現で言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: 'financial strain' や 'fiscal pressure'。economic burden = financial strain / heavy economic cost。

practiceQuestions.q: ジャヤーヴァルマン7世の統治の功罪を要約してください。
practiceQuestions.a: 功: 平和と繁栄をもたらした黄金期。罪: 大規模建設事業が経済的負担となり帝国の衰退に寄与した可能性。

practicePassage.en:
[出典: The Temples of Jayavarman VII]
While Jayavarman's time as king is considered by many to be the Khmer empire's golden age, it may also have paved the way for the empire's downfall. According to some researchers, Jayavarman's temple construction was evidence of his policy of centralizing power. As the king himself took ownership of the temples' lands, a unified, government-controlled system emerged, depriving local landowners of power. Meanwhile, construction of the temples required that tens of thousands of people relocate to cities, which meant there were far fewer people in rural areas to farm the land and produce food for the empire. Furthermore, the building projects used up a significant amount of the empire's wealth. These factors combined became a huge problem for later Khmer kings when the empire suffered because of droughts and monsoons.
practicePassage.ja: ジャヤーヴァルマンの王としての時代はクメール帝国の黄金時代と多くの人に考えられている一方で、帝国の衰退への道を切り開いた可能性もある。一部の研究者によると、ジャヤーヴァルマンの寺院建設は権力を中央集権化する政策の証拠だった。王自身が寺院の土地の所有権を取得するにつれ、統一された政府管理のシステムが出現し、地方の地主から権力を奪った。一方、寺院建設のために数万人が都市に移住する必要があり、農地を耕し帝国のための食料を生産する農村部の人々がはるかに少なくなることを意味した。さらに、建設プロジェクトは帝国の富のかなりの量を消費した。これらの要因が重なり合い、帝国が干ばつやモンスーンの被害を受けた際に、後のクメール王にとって大きな問題となった。
